message:
Fix NO_UNICODE="false" always disabling Unicode box characters

auto_output_mode() checks [[ -n "${NO_UNICODE:-}" ]] which tests for
a non-empty string. The global initializer NO_UNICODE="false" is
non-empty, so -n returns true and Unicode is unconditionally disabled,
producing ASCII +/- box chars instead of ╔═╗║╚╝╭─╮│╰╯.

Fix: initialize NO_UNICODE="" (empty = unset). The --no-unicode flag
and apply_no_unicode() still set it to "true" when requested.

Combined with the prior $'...' ANSI-C quoting fix, print_box() now
renders correctly in all invocation modes including curl | bash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -124,7 +124,7 @@ STATUS_JSON="false"
 LOG_FILE=""
 HAS_JQ="false"
 HAS_PYTHON="false"
-NO_UNICODE="false"
+NO_UNICODE=""
 
 log_to_file() {
     if [[ -n "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
